## [00:00 - 03:02]

So guys, welcome back to another module of Six Figure SMA. In today's video, I want to just go over the content timeline and how I used it to just completely revamp my content output and just my social media management services in general, I guess. So you might have seen the content timeline. It's in section 12 in the resources section. I just want to break it down for you and break down step by step how I use it. Before the content timeline, what I was doing was I was like, say a client signs on and we start first of March. First of March, it starts. So what I would do is I'd pretty much just show up back at their place like once every week or two weeks to just grab new content. And what that meant was there was so much travel time involved, so much logistics involved, all that stuff, like constantly having to find days to shoot and stuff like that. So what I did was out of desperation, I created this content timeline where I went, look, Eman, you're putting yourself under a lot of stress by trying to do it all in one month. So instead, make it a two month process. And that's what I went ahead and did. And I want to show you guys that today. As you can see right here at the content timeline, it winds up. So basically from the first, so say they start on March, from the first to the 15th of February or once again, they can sign on at any point. This I just kind of use this like as a blank slate. But usually, for example, I'll try to sign them on before a month before we actually get started and then I'll just change the dates kind of kind of to it. So let's just say from the day you have an agreement from the first to the 15th day that our agreement commences, you go ahead and you shoot all the content. So get all the content, do all the shoots. By the 16th, the content is sent. So from the first to the 16th, all the content is curated, it's shot, it's edited and the content is sent over. Now from the 16th to the 19th, the client can request re-edits or changes and they can request one round of changes to the edits. So from the 19th to the 23rd, we work on re-editing everything for them and getting that over to them. So the re-edited content is sent and the captions are also sent. So then we move into the 24th to the 27th, where basically the client will then go ahead and amend the captions and then they'll send it back to us. Then on the 27th, the invoice is sent and then IG Media Schedules content. So usually you'll actually want to send this invoice a little bit earlier. Do not post anything until the invoice is fulfilled. So usually I'll send this invoice on like the 23rd or the 24th and just as long as it's paid by the 30th, then we can get started somewhere along five, six days before the content is actually distributed now.

## [03:02 - 04:37]

Cool thing about this guys is as I said, most of the time you can just go in for one content shoot and then you can do that. It's stuff all of that at sort of the beginning or get a freelancer to do it. And the cool thing is with this is they know exactly what content is going out, they know exactly what captions are going out, they've amended it and they've made any changes that they actually want to. So you're not going to get any complaints from them in terms of content output. Before I used to put out content for clients or captions and they'd turn around and they'd go like, oh no, we don't like that. And it was a bit annoying because I really just had no idea what I was doing and content was going out and sometimes they'd like it, sometimes they wouldn't and it was just an entire process that was very draining and very daunting. So with this, by the time the 30th rolls around, as I said, say you guys come to an agreement January 27th, you start work on the 1st of February and then by the time the 30th is done, that's it, that entire month is done. Really all you have to do in an hour is you have to report and then if you're doing growth for them, you know, just manage any sort of like Insta macro and you sort of auto-liker or bots you're using for them or any sort of cheap Facebook ads you're running for them. Go ahead and do that. And also the cool thing, as I said, is this protects you because they can see you're doing a bunch of work, but they're not actually going to get any of the results, reap any of the rewards from it until the money is actually paid. So this protects you, it protects them and overall it's an amazing, amazing system. So guys, hope you enjoy it and hope you implement it as well.
